On @DEXintheCityPod, she, Jessi Brooks, and @TuongvyLe12 walk through why CME took the CFTC to court. ⚖️

tweets.text:
>Let millions in for "temporary" safety under an 18-month program
>Call any effort to end it racist
>Tie it up in courts for 15 years
>Allow them to produce hundreds of thousands of US citizens who can vote and collect taxpayer benefits for life
>Temporary is now permanent
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Absolute insanity…

The 1.3 Million people here under TPS have had nearly 400,000 anchor babies.

We let them in for *temporary* safety and obviously don’t allow them to vote in our elections, yet they have children while here who can vote and collect taxpayer benefits for life.

thread_tweets.text:
Yes conceptual thinking requires this line of thinking in your brain

1. Andy said MSTR is a CEF
2. He must know it's not literally a CEF
3. So he must mean it's like a CEF
4. Let me followup with him and confirm he is talking about a CEF as a concept. Instead of stating the literal definition of a CEF
5. Oh and yes before I state the literal definition of a CEF make sure I have that definition correct. 
Because CEF's can and often do issue more shares post IPO when MNAV is at a premium, leverage with bank loans, and debt issuance, and even on occasion issue convertible debt
6. Thank Andy for the engagement instead of claiming he is back pedaling because I was completely wrong in everything I said, lacked conceptual thinking ability and was most a dumb douchebag

tweets.text:
No one thinks AI has topped.
AI is the final frontier, 
AI is the final industry,
AI will exist in perpetuity. 

AI companies on the other hand, 
Their ancillaries on the other hand,
Their bottlenecks on the other hand, 
Can top and will top.

Both can be true simultaneously.

tweets.text:
Robotics is next. 

Both deal count and investment amounts are skyrocketing per pitchbook March data (source: a16z)

Good thing is: the same AI DC exposure often has cross-exposure to humanoid ramp.

Like DRAM/NAND with memory (on humanoid inference/storage) or DFB lasers with photonics (FMCW LiDAR vision/sensing).

Right now most exposure is upstream component parts… or programs within large players like $AMZN or $TSLA. 

So global IPO season H2 into 2027 for pure play humanoids/robotics companies is going to be fun.

tweets.text:
Kinda nutty OpenAI’s is launching its heavyweight 5.6 Sol frontier model on $CBRS.

At up to 750 tokens/sec, which is obscene performance.

American inference go brrr.

I actually picked up starter positions on Cerebras for the first time in the ~170s (below $185 IPO price) after seeing this news yesterday.

Feels like validation of its tech. IMO a bit overvalued compared to profitable companies like $JBL at the same valuation.

But there’s likely premiums for OpenAI exposure, as long as they stay the leader.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Brooo, OpenAI is back and they're releasing their new model GPT-5.6 Sol. 

With their benchmarks, OpenAI claims: 

- Sol Ultra beats Mythos 5 and mogs it for coding/workflow. 

- And is better for bio/cybersecurity too.  

Pretty bullish on OpenAI again if they leapfrogged https://t.co/Fzls4neIrI

thread_tweets.text:
pls don’t blindly follow along, esp. since I actively manage my own positions!

$CBRS is just a cautionary position for me right now. 

Cause things evolve every month in the AI space (eg. Custom jalapeño chips, maybe OpenAI has troubles, rate hikes), etc.

But if it’s compelling to you, you do you.

Ah sorry. I would have posted this idea like <10m when the new news broke.

But I don’t post new positions during market hours anymore and I have a compliance review for each of my posts, which delays things a tiny bit.

My follower count is uncomfortably high compared when I had none few months back, so need to be cautious now.

Modern self driving cars started to be a thing around 2009 with Waymo

It took 25 years to get them being viable at least in few select cities (SF, Shenzhen...)

(order of magnitude more complex machine than a self driving car) 

walking among us and serving us everywhere by 2030

tweets.text:
He explains very elloquently what many understand intuitively

Microstrategy needs "dumb money" (for lack of better word, could be retail but not necessarily) to bid up the products into premium so he can raise more money

The thing that attracts dumb money is the narrative, his messiah aura, his brand

Damage the brand bad enough, dumb money leaves, premium evaporates and with it Saylor's ability to maneuvre

tweets.text:
Casual Friday is one of the longest-running crypto trading podcasts.

You should listen to it because the hosts are extremely qualified: 

DonAlt got liquidated buying the top of XRP memecoins and ran away to New Zealand.

I last took a trade in the Great Corn Depegging of 1912 and haven't touched an orderbook since.

Enjoy.

0:00 BTC sub-60k
2:31 MicroStrategy overhang
21:10 Schizo deep dive on trading levels
37:18 ETH, HYPE, SOL
1:07:00 Why you need to gamble when you're young
1:16:31 SpaceX is trading like a shitcoin

tweets.text:
Most exciting stuff in the market right now (for me)

- We've just received strongest validation of open/decentralized ai thesis: Fable/Mythos 5 ban + Anthropic KYC terms + Gov approving GPT 5.6 release customer-by-customer. Gov gatekeeping AI → retail & enterprises opt for open/decentralized ai instead

- Dolphin AI released v2 of the network, representing real progress towards  peer-to-pool decentralized inference infra (Golang rewrite, auto updates, NVFP4 quantization, sharded inference, and a stacked pipeline like custom inference engine, decentralized RL, and more)

- Bittensor releasing new set of network upgrades, subnet owners/investors lock-stake to show "Conviction", shift from Net TaoFlow-based emission to price-based emission for fairer economic, "Root Reborn"  proposal for more productive root prop yield management, and more

- Arcium Blackthorn's Confidential AI introduces additional privacy guarantee on top of NVIDIA GPU through Arcium MPC with a single software switch

- Agentic Commerce x402 found its first real use case → pay-per-use model inference, sharp discount from compression, per use/x402 subsidy

- Data is about to become a bottleneck → Ilya Sutskever says the AI scaling era is running out of road because pre-training is about to hit a finite-data wall. 2012 - 2020 = age of research, 2020 - 2025 = age of scaling but now the scale is so big, 2026+ = back to age of research

- Grass token holders call on July 7 will answer whether rev goes to token holders/whether they're token-aligned. Story pivoting to "Data Foundation" & investing in Kled further validate the demand for data. Reppo about to validate its decentralized data evaluation network/data pipeline with enterprise clients

2026 will be the best year for open/decentralized ai and privacy
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
One of my core thesis this cycle is Ungated, Open and Decentralized AI

The thesis led me to Bittensor ecosystem early 2025

I've stuck around since then and curated a subnet portfolio out of genuine curiosity and belief that they're working on something very interesting https://t.co/sNF8KRq5hQ
tweets.full_text:
Most exciting stuff in the market right now (for me)

- We've just received strongest validation of open/decentralized ai thesis: Fable/Mythos 5 ban + Anthropic KYC terms + Gov approving GPT 5.6 release customer-by-customer. Gov gatekeeping AI → retail & enterprises opt for open/decentralized ai instead

- Dolphin AI released v2 of the network, representing real progress towards  peer-to-pool decentralized inference infra (Golang rewrite, auto updates, NVFP4 quantization, sharded inference, and a stacked pipeline like custom inference engine, decentralized RL, and more)

- Bittensor releasing new set of network upgrades, subnet owners/investors lock-stake to show "Conviction", shift from Net TaoFlow-based emission to price-based emission for fairer economic, "Root Reborn"  proposal for more productive root prop yield management, and more

- Arcium Blackthorn's Confidential AI introduces additional privacy guarantee on top of NVIDIA GPU through Arcium MPC with a single software switch

- Agentic Commerce x402 found its first real use case → pay-per-use model inference, sharp discount from compression, per use/x402 subsidy

- Data is about to become a bottleneck → Ilya Sutskever says the AI scaling era is running out of road because pre-training is about to hit a finite-data wall. 2012 - 2020 = age of research, 2020 - 2025 = age of scaling but now the scale is so big, 2026+ = back to age of research

- Grass token holders call on July 7 will answer whether rev goes to token holders/whether they're token-aligned. Story pivoting to "Data Foundation" & investing in Kled further validate the demand for data. Reppo about to validate its decentralized data evaluation network/data pipeline with enterprise clients

2026 will be the best year for open/decentralized ai and privacy

tweets.full_text: @0xFreeLunch @coffeedev very kind, thank you @0xFreeLunch 🙏🏽

tweets.text:
i’ve never seen anything like the gravitational pull openai and anthropic are currently exerting on the talent market - it’s like everyone’s thrown the towel in and accepted 2 companies will run the world going forwards:

in the last 10 days key leaders at google deepmind, meta, apple, leading universities have quit and joined them.

and it’s not just researchers - economists, scientists, psychologists, priests (even the pope!) are strategically aligning themselves with these 2 labs.

this is going to have major implications on societal hierarchies, policies/law and government going forwards - not to mentions the obvious wealth distribution

absolutely insane times
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: BREAKING: Apple’s Vision Pro and Smart Glasses chief is leaving for OpenAI’s hardware division, the latest blow to Apple’s efforts in the space. https://t.co/lNGcWGMPaC
